demographics.living_situation: two-week-old infant living at home with both parents

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: The baby will not wake up to feed, keeps vomiting everything up, and feels completely floppy. (Reported by both parents on an after-hours call about their two-week-old.)
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: Over the last day she has gone downhill fast — she won't wake to feed, she keeps throwing up, and now she feels limp and won't respond to us.

symptom_narrative.onset: a sudden, severe change over the past 24 hours
symptom_narrative.duration: getting worse over about a day; hasn't fed at all for the last 8 hours
symptom_narrative.character: very sleepy and hard to wake, floppy and not responding, throwing up repeatedly and refusing the breast
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the parents say she has never been like this — she seems 'completely floppy' and won't wake up
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: the whole baby — she is limp all over and not herself
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant and worsening; the vomiting keeps happening and she is more and more difficult to rouse
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: she keeps bringing up any feed they attempt
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: she looks blotchy and cool, the soft spot on her head looks sunken, and she isn't making tears when she cries
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: nothing like this before; she has been feeding until the last day
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: she is a newborn who is now not feeding at all and barely responding

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: When she was born the doctors said her genitals looked a bit different / not clearly formed, and we were waiting on an appointment with a specialist to look into it.
past_medical_history.when_diagnosed: noted at birth, two weeks ago
past_medical_history.how_managed: specialist review was booked but had not happened yet

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: First two weeks at home with both parents; parents are extremely frightened and made the call together. No unwell contacts at home.

paediatric_context.developmental_concerns: Two-week-old; parents report a genital difference noted at birth for which a specialist appointment was pending.

objective_data_offered.value: she is really floppy and won't wake up properly, even when we pick her up and try to rouse her
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: parent observation over the phone, not a clinician assessment

objective_data_offered.value: she keeps vomiting up everything and has refused to feed at all for about 8 hours
her skin looks blotchy/mottled and feels cool to touch
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: subjective parent description of colour and temperature

objective_data_offered.value: the soft spot on top of her head looks sunken and she isn't making any tears when she cries
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: parent-observed signs, not a clinical hydration assessment

objective_data_offered.value: the doctors said at birth her genitals looked a bit different and we were waiting to see a specialist
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: recalled from the birth admission; parents do not have a diagnosis
